opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I noticed these stark white patches on around the mouth (and a fingertip) and I'm terrified it's a flesh-eating fungus, a parasite, or that I've been burned by something.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I only noticed it tonight and it looks so abnormal that I panicked it's an infection eating my skin.

symptom_narrative.onset: noticed tonight (likely present a while, slowly progressive)
symptom_narrative.character: chalk-white patches, completely painless, no itch, smooth skin
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: no pain/itch, but very frightening to look at
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: scrubbing/washing the area (now a red rim)
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: painless, no itch, no scale; a red rim from vigorous washing/scrubbing; no fever; feeling anxious/panicky

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: no strong chemical/bleach exposure; has been scrubbing the area

objective_data_offered.value: well-defined chalk-white patches on around the mouth (and a fingertip); the skin is smooth and normal with no scale, no raised border, no blisters or open sores; a faint red rim from scrubbing that fades when pressed
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: phone-camera view; cannot perform Wood's-lamp/microscopy or test sensation remotely
